author: Munawwar Khan, Munshi
title: Hayat-i-Haidar
gloss: A biographical sketch of the life of Haidar Ali of Mysore.
notes: Haidar Ali of Mysore as Urdu biographical subject — anti-British war hero in the registered press.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 43 (follows 42 on p18)

author: Jauhar, M. S.
title: Dilfarosh
gloss: For subject see entry No. 1 at page 26 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.
notes: Jauhar's Dilfarosh (adaptation of The Merchant of Venice) in 3rd edition; cross-reference 1909Q2 entry 1, page 26.

title: Khun-i-Nahaq urf Hamlet
gloss: An adaptation of Hamlet by Mahta Tulsi Das Datt.
publisher: B. Daya Singh and Son, Book-sellers
notes: 'Khun-i-Nahaq' (Unjust Blood), alias Hamlet — Shakespeare adapted into Urdu by a Hindu (Mahta/Datt) adapter; pairs with Dilfarosh (s15) as the quarter's Shakespeare cluster.

author: Riaz-ud-din
title: Zehri Sanp urf Anjam-i-Asmat
gloss: A drama in which a slave murders a nawab and usurps his dominions, but is afterwards punished by the nawab's son.
notes: Serial margin obscured by X; read as 17 (16 precedes, 18 follows).

author: Riaz-ud-din Ahmad, Munshi
title: Kamil Zalima Shamsa urf khubsurat Bala
gloss: For subject see entry No. 26 at page 22 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1909.
publisher: Lala Ram Das and Brother, Book-sellers
copyright: Lala Ram Das, Book-seller, Lahore; No. 168, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: Hindu bookseller copyrights a Muslim-authored Urdu melodrama — commercial theatre as cross-communal property. Cross-reference 1909Q3 entry 26, page 22.

title: Chandrawali Nek Niyyat
gloss: Story noticed in entry No. 1 at page 26 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March, 1910. Transliterated by M. S. Jauhar, Lahore.
notes: Serial 19 continues URDU—DRAMA across the script subsection (same rule as Punjabi Gurmukhi/Persian). Jauhar self-transliterates his own drama into Devanagari — script-market conversion in action; cross-reference 1910Q1 entry 1, page 26.
marks: pencil '57' right margin; serial obscured by X

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 19 (continues Urdu—Drama sequence)

author: Amjad Husain, Munshi
title: Ranj Ganj
gloss: A novel in which a husband suspects the chastity of his wife, but is afterwards convinced of the purity of her conduct.

author: Gauhar Ali Khan, Gauhar
title: Kamni, almashhur Husn-i-Farang
gloss: The tragic story of the pure love of a Muhammadan gentleman and of an Englishman's wife and that of the guilty love of another Muhammadan and of a Hindu girl.
notes: 'Husn-i-Farang' (Beauty of Europe) — interracial/intercommunal romance plot, catalogued with the registrar's moral adjectives ('pure love' vs 'guilty love'); prime example of cataloguer editorializing.
